name: Build Evaluation Harness
description: Build an evaluation suite with task definition, datasets, metrics, baselines, reliability checks, and report artifacts.

inputs.description: Capability, risk, or benchmark target to evaluate.
Models, prompts, or systems to compare.
Existing dataset, task suite, or seed examples.

permissions.description: Read public eval tasks, dataset cards, model cards, and benchmark documentation.

approval_points.description: Run the evaluation suite against candidate systems.
Publish evaluation results, benchmark cards, or leaderboard data.

phases.name: Objective
phases.objective: Define capability, failure modes, target users, and scoring needs.

phases.name: Dataset And Metrics
phases.objective: Create or select examples, splits, references, metrics, measurements, and comparison tests.

phases.name: Scoring Code
phases.objective: Implement scoring adapters, task config, aggregation, and sample-level result capture.

phases.name: Baseline Systems
phases.objective: Run baseline systems and record sample-level outputs for debugging.

phases.name: Reliability
phases.objective: Check reproducibility, sensitivity, annotation quality, and metric failure modes.

phases.name: Report
phases.objective: Package eval suite, results, limitations, and optional publication notes.

required_outputs.description: Evaluation objective, users, risks, and acceptance criteria.
Evaluation dataset source, splits, references, and fingerprint.
Metrics, comparisons, measurements, aggregation, and thresholds.
Scoring code, task configuration, and adapter notes.
Baseline aggregate metrics and comparison results.
Sample-level predictions, references, scores, and metadata.
Reliability, sensitivity, and metric risk findings.

verifiers.description: Evaluation objective maps to observable examples and metrics.
Metrics, comparisons, measurements, aggregation, and thresholds are documented.
Scoring code runs on a minimal example and emits expected fields.
At least one baseline or reference system result is recorded.
Reliability, sample-level debugging, and metric failure modes were checked.
Final report claims cite metrics, sample results, and reliability notes.
